functions.name: roadtools.roadlib.metadef.database.parse_db_argument
documentation:
Parse DB string given as argument into full path required
for SQLAlchemy

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.metadef.database.SerializeMixin.as_dict
documentation: Converts the object to a dict

modules.name: roadtools.roadlib.constants
documentation: Constants for roadlib containing well-known resources, login templates (xml), etc

classes.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.loadcert
documentation: Load a device certificate from disk

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.loadkey
documentation:
Load private key only (to use as transport key)
Optionally load it as transport key only and not as device key to support separate transport keys

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.loadprt
documentation: Load PRT from disk

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.setprt
documentation: Set PRT parameters in the correct internal format

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.saveprt
documentation: Save PRT data to file

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.loadhellokey
documentation: Load Windows Hello key from file

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.get_privkey_kid
documentation: Get the kid (key ID) for the given key from a file

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.create_hello_key
documentation: Create a key for Windows Hello, saving it to a file

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.create_pubkey_blob_from_key
documentation:
Convert a key (or certificate) to RSA key blob
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/bcrypt/ns-bcrypt-bcrypt_rsakey_blob

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.create_public_jwk_from_key
documentation:
Convert a key (or certificate) to JWK public numbers
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7517

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.create_public_jwk_from_ec_key
documentation:
Convert a key (or certificate) to JWK public numbers
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7517

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.register_device
documentation: Registers or joins a device in Azure AD. Requires an access token to the device registration service.

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.register_hybrid_device
documentation:
Register hybrid device. Requires existing key/cert to be already loaded and the SID to be specified.
Device should be synced to AAD already, otherwise this will fail.
Note that certout and privout will be suffixed with the _aad suffix in the actual output to prevent overwriting the original self-signed cert

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.delete_device
documentation: Delete the device from Azure ID. Requires client cert auth

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.request_token_with_devicecert_signed_payload
documentation: Wrap the request payload in a JWT and sign this using the device cert / key

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.decrypt_jwe_with_transport_key
documentation: Decrypt JWE data structure with transport key to obtain session key

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.request_token_with_sessionkey_signed_payload
documentation:
Request a token (access / refresh / PRT) using a payload signed
with the PRT session key.

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.request_token_with_sessionkey_signed_payload_prtprotocolv3
documentation:
Request a token (access / refresh / PRT) using a payload signed
with the PRT session key. Uses PRT Protocol version 3

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.calculate_derived_key_ecdh
documentation:
Use ECDH with transport key to calculate the derived key
using Concat KDF

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.deviceauth.DeviceAuthentication.aad_brokerplugin_prt_auth
documentation: Auth using a PRT emulating the AAD Brokerplugin (WAM) client

Device authentication for ROADtools. Handles device registration,
PRT request/renew and token request using WAM emulation.

classes.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.AuthenticationException
documentation:
Generic exception we can throw when auth fails so that the error
goes back to the user.

classes.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.get_authority_url
documentation:
Returns the authority URL for the tenant specified, or the
common one if no tenant was specified

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.set_client_id
documentation: Sets client ID to use (accepts aliases)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.set_origin_value
documentation: Sets Origin header to use

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.set_resource_uri
documentation: Sets resource URI to use (accepts aliases)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.set_user_agent
documentation: Overrides user agent (accepts aliases)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.user_discovery
documentation: Discover whether this is a federated user

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.loadappcert
documentation: Load a certificate from disk for usage with application auth

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_device_code
documentation: Authenticate the end-user using device auth.

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_username_password
documentation:
Authenticate using user w/ username + password.
This doesn't work for users or tenants that have multi-factor authentication required.

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_device_code_native
documentation:
Authenticate with device code flow
Native version without adal

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_device_code_native_v2
documentation:
Authenticate with device code flow
Native version without adal

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_username_password_native
documentation:
Authenticate using user w/ username + password.
This doesn't work for users or tenants that have multi-factor authentication required.
Native version without adal

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_username_password_native_v2
documentation:
Authenticate using user w/ username + password.
This doesn't work for users or tenants that have multi-factor authentication required.
Native version without adal, for identity platform v2 endpoint

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_as_app
documentation: Authenticate with an APP id + secret (password credentials assigned to app or service principal)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_as_app_native
documentation:
Authenticate with an APP id + secret
Native ROADlib implementation

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_as_app_native_v2
documentation: Authenticate with an APP id + secret (password credentials assigned to serviceprinicpal)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.generate_federated_assertion
documentation:
Generate a federated assertion for the specified key ID, issuer, subject and audience.
Appkeypem should be a PEM encoded private key (as bytes), if not specified it should already be loaded

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_code
documentation: Authenticate with a code plus optional secret in case of a non-public app (authorization grant)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_refresh
documentation:
Authenticate with a refresh token, refreshes the refresh token
and obtains an access token

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_refresh_native
documentation:
Authenticate with a refresh token plus optional secret in case of a non-public app (authorization grant)
Native ROADlib implementation without adal requirement

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_refresh_native_v2
documentation:
Authenticate with a refresh token plus optional secret in case of a non-public app (authorization grant)
Native ROADlib implementation without adal requirement
This function calls identity platform v2 and thus requires a scope instead of resource

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_code_native
documentation:
Authenticate with a code plus optional secret in case of a non-public app (authorization grant)
Native ROADlib implementation without adal requirement - also supports PKCE

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_code_native_v2
documentation:
Authenticate with a code plus optional secret in case of a non-public app (authorization grant)
Native ROADlib implementation without adal requirement - also supports PKCE
This function calls identity platform v2 and thus requires a scope instead of resource

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_code_encrypted
documentation:
Encrypted code redemption. Like normal code flow but requires
session key to decrypt response.

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_saml_native
documentation:
Authenticate with a SAML token from the Federation Server
Native ROADlib implementation without adal requirement

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_saml_native_v2
documentation:
Authenticate with a SAML token from the Federation Server
Native ROADlib implementation without adal requirement
This function calls identity platform v2 and thus requires a scope instead of resource

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.get_desktopsso_token
documentation: Get desktop SSO token either with plain username and password, or with a Kerberos auth token

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_desktopsso_token
documentation: Authenticate with Desktop SSO token

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.build_auth_url
documentation:
Build authorize URL. Can be v2 by specifying scope, otherwise defaults
to v1 with resource

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.create_prt_cookie_kdf_ver_2
documentation: KDF version 2 cookie construction

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_prt_v2
documentation: KDF version 2 PRT auth

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_prt
documentation:
Authenticate with a PRT and given context/derived key
Uses KDF version 1 (legacy)

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.calculate_derived_key_v2
documentation: Derived key calculation v2, which uses the JWT body

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.calculate_derived_key
documentation: Calculate the derived key given a session key and optional context using KBKDFHMAC

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.decrypt_auth_response
documentation:
Decrypt an encrypted authentication response, which is a JWE
encrypted using the sessionkey

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.decrypt_auth_response_derivedkey
documentation: Decrypt an encrypted authentication response, using the derived key

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.get_srv_challenge
documentation: Request server challenge (nonce) to use with a PRT

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.get_prt_cookie_nonce
documentation:
Request a nonce to sign in with. This nonce is taken from the sign-in page, which
is how Chrome processes it, but it could probably also be obtained using the much
simpler request from the get_srv_challenge function.

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.authenticate_with_prt_cookie
documentation: Authenticate with a PRT cookie, optionally re-signing the cookie if a key is given

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.get_sub_argparse
documentation: Get an argparse subparser for authentication

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.tokenreply_to_tokendata
documentation: Convert /token reply from Azure to ADAL compatible object

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.parse_compact_jwe
documentation:
Parse compact JWE according to
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7516#section-3.1

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.parse_jwt
documentation:
Simple JWT parsing function
returns header and body as dict and signature as bytes

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.lookup_resource_uri
documentation: Translate resource URI aliases

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.lookup_client_id
documentation: Translate client ID aliases

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.lookup_user_agent
documentation: Translate user agents aliases

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.requests_get
documentation: Wrapper around requests.get to set all the options uniformly

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.requests_post
documentation: Wrapper around requests.post to set all the options uniformly

methods.name: roadtools.roadlib.auth.Authentication.get_tokens
documentation:
Get tokens based on the arguments specified.
Expects args to be generated from get_sub_argparse
Authentication class for ROADtools
